what: Read GGUF Model Files into Rfmalloc-Backed Arrays
title: Read GGUF Model Files into Rfmalloc-Backed Arrays
text: Rgguf reads GGUF model files - format used llama.cpp store large language model tensors metadata - exposes tensors directly Rfmalloc-backed, file-backed ALTREP matrices arrays, dequantizing quantized tensor types demand read. GGUF parsing done vendored, MIT-licensed copy Salvatore Sanfilippo’s (antirez’s) gguflib (src/gguflib.c, fp16.c, bf16.h); see inst/COPYRIGHTS full attribution.

title: Read GGUF Model Files into Rfmalloc-Backed Arrays
text: Allocation happens R side, filling happens C: R allocates destination Rfmalloc::create_fmalloc_matrix()/create_fmalloc_array(), returns properly classed, file-backed ALTREP object - Rfmalloc’s Ops/matrix-product dispatch already works . Native code locates requested tensor memory-mapped GGUF file writes dequantized values directly destination’s REAL() payload (ALTREP Dataptr exposes file-backed fmalloc memory directly - extra copy back ordinary R vector). GGML/GGUF tensors store dim[0] fastest-varying (contiguous) dimension. R arrays column-major (first dimension varies fastest), dim[0] maps directly onto R’s first dimension: dim(gguf_tensor(x, name)) == c(dim[0], dim[1], ...), transposition needed. verified dedicated round-trip test (inst/tinytest/test_gguf_roundtrip.R).

title: Read GGUF Model Files into Rfmalloc-Backed Arrays
Read GGUF Model Files into Rfmalloc-Backed Arrays
text: example runs inside local() .exit() cleanup stays scoped snippet README rendered; code, keep runtime handle need call Rfmalloc::cleanup_fmalloc() finished.
code: library(Rgguf)  local({   gguf_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf")   alloc_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc(alloc_file)   on.exit({     Rfmalloc::cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(c(gguf_file, alloc_file))   }, add = TRUE)    # Build a small example GGUF file (gguf_write_tensors() only emits F32   # tensors; it exists mainly to make round-trip tests possible without   # shipping binary fixtures).   set.seed(42)   gguf_write_tensors(gguf_file,     tensors = list(       w1 = matrix(rnorm(100 * 50), nrow = 100, ncol = 50),       w2 = matrix(rnorm(50 * 30), nrow = 50, ncol = 30)     ),     metadata = list(name = "example-model", version = 1)   )    # Inspect metadata and the tensor directory without reading any payload.   str(gguf_metadata(gguf_file))   print(gguf_tensors(gguf_file))    # Read tensors into Rfmalloc-backed matrices sharing one backing file.   mats <- gguf_import(gguf_file, runtime = rt)   print(Rfmalloc::is_fmalloc_vector(mats$w1))    # Matrix products dispatch through Rfmalloc and stay file-backed.   product <- mats$w1 %*% mats$w2   print(dim(product))   print(Rfmalloc::is_fmalloc_vector(product))   print(max(abs(product - as.matrix(mats$w1[, ]) %*% as.matrix(mats$w2[, ])))) }) #> List of 2 #>  $ name   : chr "example-model" #>  $ version: num 1 #>   name type n_dims    dims n_elements nbytes offset #> 1   w1  f32      2 100, 50       5000  20000    192 #> 2   w2  f32      2  50, 30       1500   6000  20192 #> [1] TRUE #> [1] 100  30 #> [1] TRUE #> [1] 0

title: Read GGUF Model Files into Rfmalloc-Backed Arrays
text: gguf_tensor(= "native") skips dequantization import: tensor’s raw GGUF payload copied fmalloc storage original density (4.5 bits/weight q4_k) returned Rfmalloc::fmalloc_tensor. Matrix products dense operands decode payload bounded, block-aligned panels streamed BLAS dgemm, full double representation never materialized. Rgguf registers gguflib’s q4_0, q4_1, q8_0, q2_k, q4_k, q6_k dequantizers Rfmalloc tensor codecs; f32, f16, bf16, f64 codecs built Rfmalloc.
code: local({   gguf_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf")   alloc_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".bin")   rt <- Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc(alloc_file)   on.exit({     Rfmalloc::cleanup_fmalloc(rt)     unlink(c(gguf_file, alloc_file))   }, add = TRUE)    set.seed(7)   gguf_write_tensors(gguf_file, list(     w = matrix(rnorm(256 * 64), nrow = 256, ncol = 64)   ))    ten <- gguf_tensor(gguf_file, "w", runtime = rt, as = "native")   print(ten)    x <- matrix(rnorm(8 * 256), nrow = 8)   y <- x %*% ten # decoded panel-by-panel inside dgemm   print(dim(y))   print(Rfmalloc::is_fmalloc_vector(y))    w_f64 <- gguf_tensor(gguf_file, "w", runtime = rt)   print(max(abs(y[] - (x %*% w_f64)[]))) }) #> <fmalloc_tensor f32 [256 x 64], 65536 payload bytes> #> [1]  8 64 #> [1] TRUE #> [1] 0

title: Read GGUF Model Files into Rfmalloc-Backed Arrays
text: gguf_tensor()/gguf_import() dequantize: Direct/streamed (intermediate buffer): f32, f16, bf16, f64, i8, i16, i32, i64. Via vendored gguflib’s gguf_tensor_to_float() (transient malloc()’d float buffer, freed immediately widening double - known v1 memory cost): q8_0, q4_0, q4_1, q2_k, q4_k, q6_k. quantized formats (q5_0, q5_1, q8_1, q3_k, q5_k, q8_k, iq* formats) implemented vendored parser; gguf_tensor() errors clearly instead guessing .

title: Read GGUF Model Files into Rfmalloc-Backed Arrays
text: vendored parser memory-maps GGUF files read/write (mmap(..., PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE, MAP_SHARED, ...)), gguf_open() requires file writable even intend read tensors .

title: Read GGUF Model Files into Rfmalloc-Backed Arrays
text: GPL (>= 2). Bundled third-party code (gguflib, fp16, bf16) remains upstream MIT license, GPL-compatible; documented separately attributed inst/COPYRIGHTS.

what: Rgguf: Read 'GGUF' Model Files into 'Rfmalloc'-Backed Arrays — Rgguf-package
title: Rgguf: Read 'GGUF' Model Files into 'Rfmalloc'-Backed Arrays — Rgguf-package
text: Rgguf reads 'GGUF' model files, file format used 'llama.cpp' project store large language model tensors metadata, exposes tensors Rfmalloc-backed, file-backed ALTREP matrices arrays. Quantized tensor types supported vendored 'gguflib' parser dequantized demand read.

title: Rgguf: Read 'GGUF' Model Files into 'Rfmalloc'-Backed Arrays — Rgguf-package
text: gguf_open Open GGUF file return context handle. gguf_metadata Read metadata key-value pairs. gguf_tensors List tensor directory data frame. gguf_tensor Read dequantize single tensor Rfmalloc-backed array. gguf_import Read tensors named list Rfmalloc-backed arrays. gguf_write_tensors Write minimal GGUF file (F32 tensors), mainly round-trip testing.

title: Rgguf: Read 'GGUF' Model Files into 'Rfmalloc'-Backed Arrays — Rgguf-package
text: Allocation happens R side: tensor destinations created Rfmalloc::create_fmalloc_matrix()/create_fmalloc_array(), returns properly classed, file-backed ALTREP object Rfmalloc's full Ops/matrix-product dispatch already working. Native code ever fills destination place (dequantizing goes); never allocates R vectors tensor size .

title: Rgguf: Read 'GGUF' Model Files into 'Rfmalloc'-Backed Arrays — Rgguf-package
text: every GGUF tensor type dequantizable: vendored 'gguflib' parser implements dequantization f32, f16, bf16, f64, plain integer types, q8_0, q4_0, q4_1, q2_k, q4_k, q6_k quantized block formats. quantized formats (e.g. q5_0, q5_1, q3_k, q5_k, q8_k, iq* formats) supported gguf_tensor errors clearly . vendored 'gguflib' parser memory-maps GGUF files read/write, gguf_open requires file writable even reading tensors . Dequantizing quantized tensor type currently goes intermediate malloc()'d float buffer (freed immediately widening double); future version dequantize block--block directly destination.

title: Rgguf: Read 'GGUF' Model Files into 'Rfmalloc'-Backed Arrays — Rgguf-package
text: Maintainer: Sounkou Mahamane Toure sounkoutoure@gmail.com contributors: Salvatore Sanfilippo (gguf-tools/gguflib) [copyright holder] Georgi Gerganov (GGUF format enums/structures adapted ggml) [copyright holder]

title: Import GGUF Tensors — gguf_import
text: Reads tensors GGUF file named list Rfmalloc-backed matrices/arrays, dequantizing needed. thin convenience wrapper around repeated gguf_tensor() calls shares single open file handle across .

title: Import GGUF Tensors — gguf_import
code: gguf_import(   path_or_ctx,   tensors = NULL,   runtime = NULL,   as = c("numeric", "native") )

title: Import GGUF Tensors — gguf_import
text: path_or_ctx Either path GGUF file, "gguf_ctx" object returned gguf_open(). tensors Optional character vector tensor names import. NULL (default), every tensor file imported. runtime Optional Rfmalloc runtime handle passed every gguf_tensor() call, imported tensors share backing file. NULL, Rfmalloc's default-runtime resolution used tensor. Passed gguf_tensor(): "numeric" dequantizes fmalloc double matrices/arrays, "native" keeps GGUF payload encoding returns typed fmalloc_tensor objects.

title: Import GGUF Tensors — gguf_import
text: named list Rfmalloc-backed matrices/arrays, one per imported tensor, order requested (file order, tensors NULL).

title: Import GGUF Tensors — gguf_import
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf") gguf_write_tensors(tmp, list(     w1 = matrix(as.double(1:12), nrow = 4, ncol = 3),     w2 = matrix(as.double(1:6), nrow = 3, ncol = 2) )) rt <- Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin")) mats <- gguf_import(tmp, runtime = rt) prod <- mats$w1 %*% mats$w2 Rfmalloc::is_fmalloc_vector(prod) #> [1] TRUE unlink(tmp)

title: Read GGUF Metadata — gguf_metadata
text: Reads every metadata key-value pair GGUF file named R list.

title: Read GGUF Metadata — gguf_metadata
Read GGUF Metadata — gguf_metadata
text: x Either path GGUF file, "gguf_ctx" object returned gguf_open().

title: Read GGUF Metadata — gguf_metadata
text: named list metadata values, file order.

title: Read GGUF Metadata — gguf_metadata
text: Scalar values converted closest native R type (integer 8/16/32-bit signed unsigned--int-safe integers, numeric 32-bit unsigned 64-bit integers/floats, logical booleans, character strings). Arrays supported scalar type become R vector type. metadata value type package represent (none current GGUF spec, check defensive) returned NULL keeping name, rather raising error.

title: Read GGUF Metadata — gguf_metadata
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf") gguf_write_tensors(tmp, list(w = matrix(1:6 + 0.5, nrow = 2)),     metadata = list(name = "example", version = 1)) gguf_metadata(tmp) #> $name #> [1] "example" #>  #> $version #> [1] 1 #>  unlink(tmp)

title: Open a GGUF File — gguf_open
text: Opens 'GGUF' model file returns lightweight context handle used gguf_metadata(), gguf_tensors(), gguf_tensor(), gguf_import(). file memory-mapped vendored 'gguflib' parser unmapped automatically returned object garbage collected, earlier call gguf_import()/friends plain file path (open close short-lived context internally).

title: Open a GGUF File — gguf_open
Open a GGUF File — gguf_open
text: path Character string giving path GGUF file.

title: Open a GGUF File — gguf_open
text: object class "gguf_ctx": external pointer underlying parser context, finalizer closes .

title: Open a GGUF File — gguf_open
text: vendored parser memory-maps file read/write (mmap(..., PROT_READ | PROT_WRITE, MAP_SHARED, ...)), path must point writable file even intend read tensors .

title: Open a GGUF File — gguf_open
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf") gguf_write_tensors(tmp, list(w = matrix(1:6 + 0.5, nrow = 2))) ctx <- gguf_open(tmp) gguf_tensors(ctx) #>   name type n_dims dims n_elements nbytes offset #> 1    w  f32      2 2, 3          6     24     96 unlink(tmp)

what: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
title: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
text: Reads single named tensor GGUF file, dequantizing needed, fresh Rfmalloc-backed ALTREP matrix array doubles.

title: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
code: gguf_tensor(x, name, runtime = NULL, as = c("numeric", "native"))

title: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
text: x Either path GGUF file, "gguf_ctx" object returned gguf_open(). name Tensor name, appears gguf_tensors()'s name column. runtime Optional Rfmalloc runtime handle (see Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc()). NULL, Rfmalloc's default-runtime resolution used. "numeric" (default) dequantizes whole tensor fmalloc double matrix/array. "native" instead copies tensor's raw, still-encoded payload fmalloc storage returns Rfmalloc::create_fmalloc_tensor() typed tensor: keeps GGUF storage density (e.g. 4.5 bits/weight q4_k) decoded bounded panels used matrix products. Native mode requires 2-dimensional tensor whose type registered Rfmalloc codec.

title: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
text: = "numeric", Rfmalloc-backed ALTREP matrix (tensor 2 dimensions) array (otherwise) doubles, dim() equal tensor's GGUF dimensions c(dim[0], dim[1], ...) order. = "native", fmalloc_tensor dims.

title: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
text: GGUF tensor dimensions stored dim[0] fastest-varying (contiguous) dimension. R arrays column-major, .e. first dimension varies fastest, dim[0] maps directly onto R's first dimension transposition needed: dim(result) == c(dim[0], dim[1], ...). See inst/tinytest/test_gguf_roundtrip.R test verifies mapping writing known matrices/arrays reading back.

title: Read and Dequantize a GGUF Tensor — gguf_tensor
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf") gguf_write_tensors(tmp, list(w = matrix(1:6 + 0.5, nrow = 2))) rt <- Rfmalloc::open_fmalloc(tempfile(fileext = ".bin")) w <- gguf_tensor(tmp, "w", runtime = rt) w #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]  1.5  3.5  5.5 #> [2,]  2.5  4.5  6.5 #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "fmalloc_matrix" "matrix"         "fmalloc"        "numeric"        Rfmalloc::is_fmalloc_vector(w) #> [1] TRUE unlink(tmp)

title: List GGUF Tensors — gguf_tensors
text: Lists tensor directory GGUF file data frame, without reading dequantizing tensor payload.

title: List GGUF Tensors — gguf_tensors
List GGUF Tensors — gguf_tensors
text: x Either path GGUF file, "gguf_ctx" object returned gguf_open().

title: List GGUF Tensors — gguf_tensors
text: data frame one row per tensor columns: name Tensor name. type GGUF tensor type name, e.g. "f32", "q4_k". n_dims Number dimensions. dims list column; dims[[]] integer vector tensor 's dimensions, GGUF-order (dim[0] first, fastest-varying/ contiguous dimension, gguf_tensor() maps R's first, column-major dimension). n_elements Total number scalar elements. nbytes Size tensor's raw (possibly quantized) -disk representation, bytes. offset Byte offset tensor data start file.

title: List GGUF Tensors — gguf_tensors
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf") gguf_write_tensors(tmp, list(w = matrix(1:6 + 0.5, nrow = 2), b = 1:3 + 0.5)) gguf_tensors(tmp) #>   name type n_dims dims n_elements nbytes offset #> 1    w  f32      2 2, 3          6     24    128 #> 2    b  f32      1    3          3     12    160 unlink(tmp)

what: Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
title: Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
text: Writes named list numeric vectors/matrices/arrays GGUF file 32-bit floating point (f32) tensors, optional simple metadata. minimal writer, primarily meant build small GGUF fixtures package's round-trip tests without shipping binary test fixtures, exported since also useful producing test fixtures GGUF consumers.

title: Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
text: path Character string giving output file path. tensors non-empty named list numeric vectors, matrices, arrays. Names become tensor names must unique non-empty. metadata named list metadata key-value pairs write. value must single (length-1), non-missing string numeric value; numeric values written 64-bit floats (FLOAT64). Defaults list() (metadata).

title: Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
text: path, invisibly.

title: Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
text: Tensor dimensions taken object's dim() (length() plain vector, written 1-dimensional tensor) stored GGUF dim[0]-fastest-varying order directly R's column-major storage, gguf_tensor()/gguf_import() read back exactly matrix/array wrote (see inst/tinytest/test_gguf_roundtrip.R). Existing files path silently overwritten.

title: Write a Minimal GGUF File — gguf_write_tensors
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".gguf") gguf_write_tensors(tmp,     tensors = list(weight = matrix(1:6 + 0.5, nrow = 2)),     metadata = list(name = "example-model", version = 1) ) gguf_tensors(tmp) #>     name type n_dims dims n_elements nbytes offset #> 1 weight  f32      2 2, 3          6     24    160 unlink(tmp)

title: Rgguf 0.1.0 (unreleased)
text: gguf_tensor(= "native") longer requires gguflib dequantization support tensor’s type - native import copies raw, still-encoded payload, needs registered Rfmalloc codec (another package may provide: Rllm registers GGML-backed codecs q5_0/ q5_1/q3_k/q5_k, common real Q4_K_M model files). gguflib-support check now applies = "numeric", whose error message points native path. Fixed Q4_K dequantization bug inherited upstream gguf-tools: gguf_q4_k_to_float(), high-nibble half every 64-weight group (odd sub-blocks 1, 3, 5, 7 super-block) dequantized previous sub-block’s scale/min, making q4_k decodes ~33% wrong relative Frobenius norm (GGML’s reference uses scale +0 low nibbles +1 high nibbles). Since Q4_K_M common real-model quantization, affected gguf_tensor(= "numeric") fmalloc_tensor_materialize() typical GGUF files. six registered quantized codecs (q4_0, q4_1, q8_0, q2_k, q4_k, q6_k) now decode bit-identically GGML’s type-traits to_float reference, verified new regression test (test_gguf_codec_ggml_ref.R) committed fixture GGML-quantized payloads GGML-decoded expected values. Added native typed imports: gguf_tensor(= "native") (gguf_import(= "native")) copy 2-d tensor’s raw GGUF payload fmalloc storage original density (e.g. 4.5 bits/weight q4_k) return Rfmalloc::fmalloc_tensor, decoded bounded panels inside matrix products. Registers gguflib’s q4_0/q4_1/q8_0/q2_k/ q4_k/q6_k dequantizers Rfmalloc tensor codecs (requires Rfmalloc C API version 4). Initial release. Added gguf_open() memory-map GGUF file (via vendored copy antirez’s gguflib) return gguf_ctx handle closing finalizer. Added gguf_metadata() read metadata key-value pairs (scalars arrays supported GGUF value types) named R list. Added gguf_tensors() list tensor directory (name, type, number dimensions, dimensions, element count, byte size, file offset) data frame, without reading tensor payload. Added gguf_tensor() read dequantize single named tensor directly Rfmalloc-backed, file-backed ALTREP matrix/array doubles: R allocates destination via Rfmalloc::create_fmalloc_matrix()/create_fmalloc_array(), native code fills place, ordinary R-sized copy tensor ever materialized R heap. Added gguf_import() read tensors file named list Rfmalloc-backed matrices/arrays sharing one open file handle. Added gguf_write_tensors(), minimal GGUF writer (F32 tensors plus simple string/numeric metadata), primarily build small GGUF fixtures package’s round-trip tests without shipping binary fixtures. Verified (dedicated round-trip test) GGUF’s dim[0] (fastest-varying/contiguous dimension) maps directly onto R’s first, column-major dimension, tensor dimensions need transposition: dim(gguf_tensor(x, name)) == c(dim[0], dim[1], ...). Direct (non-quantized) dequantization support f32, f16, bf16, f64, plain i8/i16/i32/i64 integer tensor types, plus quantized-block dequantization (via vendored gguflib’s gguf_tensor_to_float()) q8_0, q4_0, q4_1, q2_k, q4_k, q6_k. quantized formats (q5_0, q5_1, q3_k, q5_k, q8_k, iq* formats) implemented vendored parser gguf_tensor() errors clearly instead guessing. Documented (README, inst/smoke_test.R) discovered Rfmalloc 0.1.0 limitation, Rgguf bug: %*%/crossprod/tcrossprod fmalloc-backed operands >= 64 elements currently fail operand passed R closure call (effectively always), due generic R-core ALTREP attribute-wrapping behavior Rfmalloc unwrap. gguf_tensor()/gguf_import() unaffected size. Rgguf never calls export.
